# Findings — indexer drops wiki frontmatter behind the auto-generated HTML comment

Surfaced 2026-06-28 while implementing #266 (clickable citation links). Local
verification against the **real baked `.kb/index.json`** caught a class of latent
bugs the unit tests had been masking. This doc is the alignment artifact before
slicing the fix into issues.

## Root cause

`indexer._split_frontmatter` (the YAML-frontmatter splitter, `markdown_kb/app/indexer.py:350`)
only parses frontmatter when the file **starts with `---\n`**:

```python
if not text.startswith("---\n"):
    return {}, text          # <-- bails: no metadata, whole file becomes "body"
```

But every wiki page produced by `POST /ingest` starts with an auto-generated
sentinel **HTML comment** before the frontmatter fence:

```
<!-- Auto-generated by POST /ingest on 2026-05-29T13:34:49Z.
     Source of truth: docs/退款政策.md. ... -->

---
id: 退款政策
type: concept
sources:
- 退款政策.md#退款政策
status: live
---

# 退款政策
...
```

So `text.startswith("---\n")` is **False** → the splitter returns `({}, whole_text)`.
The frontmatter (including `type`, `sources`, `status`) is never parsed at index
time, and the comment + YAML are folded into the **body**.

### Evidence (measured)

- `parse_markdown("wiki/concepts/退款政策.md")` → section `metadata = {'type': None, 'lang': 'zh'}`.
- Baked `.kb/index.json`: 160 section `metadata` blocks, **160 carry only `lang`**, only **1** `"type":` in the entire file.
- Tests pass because the fixtures (`_write_concept_page`, gateway `tests/fixtures/docs/`) write frontmatter **starting directly with `---`** — i.e. they do NOT reproduce the real `/ingest` output shape. The test corpus diverged from production.

## Impact (why this is a tail-end "major bug")

1. **`derived_from` provenance is silently broken in production.**
   `retrieval._derived_from_for_section` reads `sec.metadata["type"]` to locate the
   parent wiki page. With `type` absent it returns `None` for every real page, so
   the reader UI's "Derived from …" docs chain (Phase 6, ADR-0006) never renders.
2. **BM25 retrieval pollution (to confirm).** Because the frontmatter + sentinel
   comment are treated as body, YAML tokens (`type`, `concept`, `status`, `live`,
   dates, hashes) are candidates for the BM25 index — noise in the scoring corpus.
   Needs a measured check of `doc_freq` after the fix.
3. **#266 clickable citations cannot work as built.** The `path` field added in the
   #266 WIP derives the wiki subdir from `sec.metadata["type"]`; with `type` absent
   it is always `None`, so citations never become clickable against the real index.

## Fix direction

Make the frontmatter splitter (and any peer that checks `startswith("---")`) **skip
a leading HTML comment** before looking for the `---` fence. Precedent already
exists in the codebase: `_derived_from_for_section` already skips this exact
sentinel comment when it reads a parent page's frontmatter.

Consequence: the parser change alters what enters the BM25 corpus, so the committed
`.kb/index.json` seed must be **re-baked** (BM25 `build_index()` — LLM-free, no cost)
and retrieval re-validated (thresholds, negative-case runner; Phase 8 comparison if
warranted). The RAG FAISS index is **unaffected** (it indexes `docs/`, which has no
sentinel comment).

## Proposed slices (for alignment — see the issues once agreed)

| Slice | Scope | Depends on |
|---|---|---|
| **S1** | Indexer: parse frontmatter behind a leading HTML comment. Tests with comment-prefixed fixtures matching real `/ingest` output. Assert `type`/`sources` now captured, `derived_from` populates, and frontmatter is NOT tokenized into BM25. | — |
| **S2** | Re-bake committed `.kb/index.json` + retrieval re-validation (BM25 scores, thresholds, negative-case runner). Confirm no regression. | S1 |
| **S3** | #266 clickable citation links — the `path` field + in-page viewer (the current `fix/266-clickable-citation-links` WIP), now resolving via real `type` metadata (drop any workaround). | S1 (+S2 for a clean served index) |
| **S4** (opt) | End-to-end regression that "Derived from …" provenance renders in the reader UI (closes the latent Phase-6 gap explicitly). | S1 |

## Test-gap lesson

Fixtures must reflect real `/ingest` output (sentinel comment + frontmatter). The
divergence is why prod was broken under a green suite. S1 should add the
comment-prefixed shape to the shared fixtures so the whole suite exercises the real
parser path.
